jokes.text: For realskies?
jokes.setup: Serious conversation about marriage intentions
jokes.punchline: Andy's childish made-up word 'realskies' in response to serious topic
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Andy's childish vocabulary contrasts beautifully with the serious marriage discussion. Economy is excellent - maximum character revelation per syllable.

jokes.text: [gasps] Ohhhhhhhhhhhh.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to find one word to sum up her feelings about Andy and Ann
jokes.punchline: Instead of a word, she just makes an extended uncomfortable sound
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie overthinking moment. The setup promises a word but delivers an awkward sound - solid misdirection.

jokes.text: Because I didn't think March 31st existed. ♪ 30 days has September, April, March, and November ♪
jokes.setup: April explains why she scheduled all meetings for March 31st
jokes.punchline: She genuinely believed the date didn't exist and misremembered the rhyme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. April's deadpan delivery of something genuinely stupid feels perfectly authentic. The wrong rhyme is the perfect cherry on top.

jokes.text: June and November.
jokes.setup: April sings wrong version of the rhyme
jokes.punchline: Leslie automatically corrects her while missing the bigger problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - she corrects the minor detail while the major catastrophe looms. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: To me, this situation is a blood-soaked, nightmarish hellscape. However, to Leslie Knope... Oh, how fun. Yay.
jokes.setup: Ron's talking head describing the 93 meetings crisis
jokes.punchline: The extreme contrast between his horror and Leslie's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron voice - the dramatic 'blood-soaked hellscape' contrasted with the flat delivery of Leslie's reaction. Quote-worthy and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Why don't I try to rustle up seven more meetings 'cause I think it would be cooler if there were an even 100.
jokes.setup: Leslie facing 93 overwhelming meetings
jokes.punchline: Her solution is to make the problem worse for aesthetic reasons
jokes.explanation: Pure Leslie logic - turning a crisis into an opportunity for round numbers. Perfectly captures her optimistic insanity.

jokes.text: Fortunately, there were two survivors. Unfortunately, they were both horses.
jokes.setup: Tragic historical story about a wedding that became a bloodbath
jokes.punchline: The only survivors were horses, not people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy structure. 'Fortunately' sets up hope, 'unfortunately' delivers the absurd punchline. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: Don't throw things at me. Oh, these are tight.
jokes.setup: Leslie urgently trying to coordinate while putting on boots
jokes.punchline: She accidentally hits Tom while struggling with tight boots
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy. Functional but not particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm actually here to see mark. Right. Oh, well, thanks for the coffee. That's also for mark. Mm. I really need it though. But next time more sugar. Okay, thanks. Bye!
jokes.setup: Ann brings coffee for Mark
jokes.punchline: Leslie obliviously takes it and gives feedback on coffee meant for someone else
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Leslie's oblivious selfishness is perfectly in character. The 'more sugar' note adds insult to injury.

jokes.text: Pre-zit. Do you have any, uh, brown concealer by any chance?
jokes.setup: Tom preparing to look good for Jessica
jokes.punchline: He's worried about a pimple that hasn't formed yet and needs specific concealer
jokes.explanation: Great Tom vanity moment. 'Pre-zit' is a clever coinage, and asking Leslie for brown concealer shows his self-absorption.

jokes.text: Attack by Dennis Feinstein. When you want to attack the senses of the lady you want to bed.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks about Tom's cologne
jokes.punchline: The cologne name and Tom's creepy marketing description
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy. The cologne name is clever, and Tom's description is perfectly sleazy in character.

jokes.text: Bert Macklin, FBI, on the case.
jokes.setup: Andy being assigned to handle meetings
jokes.punchline: He creates an FBI persona for a mundane task
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - turning boring work into an adventure. Bert Macklin becomes a beloved recurring character.

jokes.text: Just sit there and don't ruin the city.
jokes.setup: Ron giving Ann simple meeting instructions
jokes.punchline: His extremely low expectations delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan. The bar is impossibly low yet somehow feels reasonable coming from him.

jokes.text: When April was born, I was already in 3rd grade. Which means if we were friends back then, I'd have been hanging out with a baby. I don't know anything about infant care. My God, I could've killed her.
jokes.setup: Andy realizing the age gap between him and April
jokes.punchline: His escalating worry about hypothetically babysitting baby April
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy logic. The escalation from age gap to baby friendship to accidental infanticide is perfectly absurd yet feels genuine to his thought process.

jokes.text: Tell me straight. Are you a Chinese?
jokes.setup: Formal introduction between Tom and the rich old man
jokes.punchline: Casual racism delivered as polite conversation
jokes.explanation: Effective character establishment - shows Newport's casual prejudice. The grammar 'a Chinese' adds to the offensiveness.

jokes.text: That floppy old bag of money is gonna be dead in, like, a month. And who's going to comfort Jessica and her millions of dollars? Yeah, Jessica's a gold digger, but I'm a gold digger digger.
jokes.setup: Tom's talking head about Jessica and her husband
jokes.punchline: 'Gold digger digger' - he's attracted to gold diggers
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tom moment. 'Gold digger digger' is a clever construction that perfectly captures his scheming nature and twisted logic.

jokes.text: Well, I was doing a ribbon cutting at the hospital. And he was there because his blood doesn't work.
jokes.setup: Jessica explaining how she met her husband
jokes.punchline: Casual description of a serious medical condition as 'blood doesn't work'
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist medical description. Jessica's vapid delivery makes a serious condition sound trivial and funny.

jokes.text: That's a saying everywhere. I've never heard it before And I think it's a great saying.
jokes.setup: Jessica uses the common phrase 'what's done is done'
jokes.punchline: Tom contradicts himself by saying it's universal then claiming he's never heard it
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's tendency to contradict himself when trying to impress someone. Solid character moment but not exceptional.

jokes.text: History is important. You just can't go around changing everything all the time or else next thing you know They'll be painting the white house... Not white. I'm so angry, I can't think of another color. Green.
jokes.setup: Leslie making an impassioned speech about preserving history
jokes.punchline: Her anger makes her brain malfunction, can't think of colors
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing how passion makes her malfunction. Tom's helpful 'Green' is a nice punctuation.

jokes.text: I'm afraid that Ron Swanson's currently dead. Oh. I'm his daughter. April Swanson.
jokes.setup: Citizen wanting to meet with Ron Swanson
jokes.punchline: April casually announces Ron's death and claims to be his daughter
jokes.explanation: Perfect April move - completely absurd lie delivered with total deadpan conviction. The casual death announcement is brilliantly dark.

jokes.text: You won me over. I will join your team. I'm sorry, um, what we need-- When does practice start? And do you provide the jerseys? What color are the jerseys by the way? What's our team name? Are we 'the lightning'?
jokes.setup: Ultimate frisbee representative trying to explain their needs
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately assumes he's being recruited and gets excited
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy enthusiasm and self-centeredness. The rapid-fire questions show his genuine excitement about joining 'the lightning.'

jokes.text: Can you see this? Every time.
jokes.setup: Ann reveals she's not actually a parks employee but a nurse
jokes.punchline: Citizen immediately asks her to look at a medical issue
jokes.explanation: Predictable but functional joke about how people always ask medical professionals for free advice. Ann's 'every time' shows this happens constantly.

jokes.text: Your husband's a mushy, old, pile of rot.
jokes.setup: Jessica calls the gazebo a mushy old pile of rot
jokes.punchline: Leslie fires back with the same insult about Jessica's husband
jokes.explanation: Simple comeback humor. Shows Leslie losing her temper but the 'I know you are but what am I' structure is fairly basic.

jokes.text: I am not a stick in the mud! I-I just want to stop a party from happening.
jokes.setup: Leslie defending herself against being called a stick in the mud
jokes.punchline: Her defense actually proves the point - she wants to stop a party
jokes.explanation: Good self-defeating argument. Leslie's passionate defense accidentally proves Jessica's point about her being a stick in the mud.

jokes.text: I saw your penis.
jokes.setup: Leslie accidentally walks in on Ron in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Her blunt, uncomfortable announcement to the councilman
jokes.explanation: Peak awkwardness delivered with Leslie's characteristic over-sharing. The economy is perfect - maximum discomfort in three words.

jokes.text: Just say out loud 'yes' or 'no'. Eeeeeee-boop!
jokes.setup: Citizen frustrated with Andy's inability to give a straight answer
jokes.punchline: Andy makes robot noises instead of saying yes or no
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy - when asked for simple yes/no, he makes nonsense sounds. The 'eeeeeee-boop' is perfectly childish.

jokes.text: I have no idea what I was saying.
jokes.setup: Ann giving confident advice about city bureaucracy
jokes.punchline: Immediate talking head confession that she was completely clueless
jokes.explanation: Great mockumentary moment. The immediate cut to her confession of ignorance undermines her authority perfectly.

jokes.text: Kindly get your groin off my desk.
jokes.setup: Citizen asking about swimsuit appropriateness
jokes.punchline: Ron's polite but firm request about inappropriate desk usage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron politeness in an inappropriate situation. 'Kindly' makes it even funnier - he's being gentlemanly about groin removal.

jokes.text: April was supposed to be the moat that kept the citizen barbarians away from Swanson castle. Instead, she blew up the castle and stabbed me in the face.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining April's job performance
jokes.punchline: Medieval metaphor escalates to her destroying everything and attacking him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron metaphor. The medieval castle imagery is perfect for his character, and the escalation from moat to explosion to stabbing is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Hey, sorry, dude. I don't need your sympathy. Or yours.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to comfort April after Ron yelled at her
jokes.punchline: April rejects sympathy from Andy and the documentary camera
jokes.explanation: Good April moment - she's too proud to accept comfort. The camera acknowledgment breaks the fourth wall effectively.

jokes.text: Gazebo? More like 'Ga-zoinks-Bo.' She may be a former beauty queen, but today she's the king of destroying history.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to create a catchphrase for her news interview
jokes.punchline: Terrible wordplay and mixed metaphors about Jessica
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad wordplay that shows Leslie's weakness at being clever under pressure. The 'king' metaphor confusion adds to the failure.

jokes.text: Crrr-AP on the cob.
jokes.setup: Leslie realizes the newspaper won't help because Sweetums owns it
jokes.punchline: Her creative censoring of her own swearing
jokes.explanation: Creative Leslie censorship. The 'on the cob' addition makes it distinctly hers - wholesome even when frustrated.

jokes.text: Really thought that gate would open in the middle.
jokes.setup: Leslie chains herself to a gate, Tom presumably crashes into it
jokes.punchline: Tom's matter-of-fact observation about his misjudgment
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy payoff. Tom's casual delivery of his mistake is mildly funny but not exceptional.

jokes.text: It never gets old!
jokes.setup: Tom apparently making some repetitive joke or gesture
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm for his own recurring bit
jokes.explanation: Generic running gag moment. Tom's self-satisfaction is in character but the joke itself isn't particularly strong.

jokes.text: Either you hire her back or I quit. You don't work for me. And I never will, sir.
jokes.setup: Andy threatening to quit unless April is rehired
jokes.punchline: Ron pointing out Andy doesn't work there, Andy doubling down
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Andy's misunderstanding. His commitment to the bit even after being corrected shows his loyalty to April.

jokes.text: Well, welcome to castle Ludgate!
jokes.setup: Ron visiting April's house
jokes.punchline: April's mom's enthusiastic greeting contrasts with April's personality
jokes.explanation: Basic contrast humor between April's goth persona and her normal family. Functional but not particularly inspired.

jokes.text: You must be April's sister. I'm Ron.
jokes.setup: Ron meets what's presumably April's young sister
jokes.punchline: He mistakes her for April's sister when she's likely much younger
jokes.explanation: Simple age-based misunderstanding. Shows Ron's social awkwardness but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: It was supposed to be me and Ann, or me and Jessica, or Ann and Jessica with me watching.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why he's upset about marriages
jokes.punchline: His fantasy scenarios escalate to voyeurism
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tom's creepy side. The progression from relationship to threesome is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I know you're probably thinking 'there's that crazy gate lady from outside back again.' And to some extent, you're right. I am the gate lady.
jokes.setup: Leslie crashing the birthday party to apologize
jokes.punchline: She acknowledges and embraces her 'gate lady' reputation
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment. Leslie owning the 'gate lady' identity shows growth and self-awareness.

jokes.text: My mom has all your albums. I recognized you the day we met.
jokes.setup: April casually mentions 'Duke Silver' as Ron leaves
jokes.punchline: She's known about Ron's secret jazz career all along
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment. April casually revealing she's known Ron's secret all along is perfectly in character and adds depth to their relationship.

jokes.text: How about June 50th? You should come back today at 2:65? He's available then. Looks like the only other day he has open is March-tember one-teenth.
jokes.setup: Citizen trying to reschedule meeting with Ron
jokes.punchline: April creates impossible fake dates, returning to her original incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the opening premise. April learned nothing and returns to creative scheduling incompetence. 'March-tember one-teenth' is inspired nonsense.
